questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to create an inline comment on selected text or at the cursor position?

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + C
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + C is used for copying text.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + M
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + M is the correct shortcut to open the comment menu.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + N
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + N is typically used for creating a new page or window depending on the OS.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + L
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + L is used for toggling a link.

questions.question: How do you resolve a comment on a page?

options.text: Hover over it and select the trash icon
options.explanation: The trash icon is for deleting the comment entirely.

options.text: Hover over it and select the checkmark (✔️)
options.explanation: The checkmark icon is used to resolve a comment.

options.text: Hover over it and select the edit icon
options.explanation: The edit icon is used to modify the comment text.

options.text: Click the comment and select 'Resolve' from the menu
options.explanation: The checkmark icon is the direct action to resolve.

questions.question: When you select text across multiple blocks, what is the behavior regarding commenting?

options.text: You can select them all and leave a comment at once
options.explanation: You cannot comment on multiple blocks simultaneously, even if text is selected across them.

options.text: You must comment on each block individually
options.explanation: You can only comment on one block or section at a time, even if text is selected across blocks.

options.text: The comment will only appear on the first block selected
options.explanation: The comment will not appear on any of the selected text if it covers multiple blocks.

options.text: You can only comment on text, not blocks
options.explanation: You can comment on specific content blocks as well as selected text.

questions.question: What is the difference between @-mentioning a page and adding a 'Link to page' block?

options.text: A Link to page creates a backlink, while @-mentioning does not
options.explanation: A backlink is created automatically whenever you @-mention a page.

options.text: @-mentioning creates a subpage in the sidebar, while Link to page acts like a hyperlink
options.explanation: Link to page creates a subpage in the sidebar; @-mentioning acts like a hyperlink.

options.text: A Link to page updates automatically if the title changes, while @-mentioning does not
options.explanation: The title of a page updates automatically wherever it is @-mentioned.

options.text: @-mentioning creates a subpage in the sidebar, while Link to page acts like a hyperlink
options.explanation: Link to page works like creating a subpage, while @-mentioning works like a hyperlink.

questions.question: How can you see a complete list of members in a group if the hover preview only shows 10?

options.text: Click the group name in the sidebar
options.explanation: You need to go into Settings to see the full list.

options.text: Search for the group in the global search
options.explanation: Global search does not list group members.

options.text: Go to Settings in your sidebar → Members → Select Groups
options.explanation: This is the correct path to view the complete list of group members.

options.text: Click the expand arrow in the mention menu
options.explanation: The mention menu is limited to 10 members as stated in the article.

questions.question: How do you leave a comment directly on a database property?

options.text: Type @ in the property field
options.explanation: Typing @ usually mentions a person or page.

options.text: Hover over the property and select the comment icon (💬)
options.explanation: Hovering over a property in table view allows you to select the comment icon to leave a property comment.

options.text: Select the property and press Cmd/Ctrl + M
options.explanation: The shortcut applies to inline comments, not necessarily property comments.

options.text: Click the property name and choose Comment
options.explanation: You usually interact with properties via hover or editing, not a 'Comment' menu on the property name.

questions.question: What happens when you type '@today' in a page?

options.text: It creates a link to today's date
options.explanation: It creates a timestamp, not a link.

options.text: It adds a timestamp for today
options.explanation: Typing @ followed by 'today' adds a timestamp for the current date.

options.text: It marks the text as urgent
options.explanation: Notion does not have a built-in 'urgent' text format.

options.text: It deletes the text
options.explanation: The text remains and becomes a timestamp.

questions.question: In the comments pane, how do you filter comments by status (Open vs Resolved)?

options.text: Click the filter icon and select status
options.explanation: The filter icon allows you to filter by person or status.

options.text: Use the Sort dropdown
options.explanation: The Sort dropdown orders by most recent reply, not by status.

options.text: Right-click the comment and select 'Filter'
options.explanation: Filtering is done via the pane's top toolbar.

options.text: Check a box in the pane header
options.explanation: There is no checkbox in the pane header for filtering.

questions.question: When using the comments pane, how is the sorting of comments handled?

options.text: It stays sorted by newest reply
options.explanation: Once opened, the pane keeps the current sort; you must close and reopen to re-sort.

options.text: It automatically re-sorts every time you open it
options.explanation: It does not auto-re-sort; it maintains the current view.

options.text: It is sorted alphabetically by author
options.explanation: It is sorted by most recent reply by default.

options.text: It sorts by the date the page was created
options.explanation: Sorting is based on activity within the comments pane.

questions.question: How do you add an emoji reaction to a comment?

options.text: Select the comment and press Cmd/Ctrl + E
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + E is used for editing.

options.text: Hover over the comment and click the smiley face (🙂)
options.explanation: Hovering over a comment reveals the smiley face for adding reactions.

options.text: Click the comment text and select an emoji from the menu
options.explanation: Reactions are added via the specific reaction icon, not the comment text menu.

options.text: Double-click the comment
options.explanation: Double-clicking is not the method for adding reactions.

questions.question: You mention a colleague in a page that they do not have access to. What happens?

options.text: They receive a notification in their Inbox
options.explanation: If they don't have access to the page, they will not be notified.

options.text: They receive an email notification
options.explanation: They will not receive any notification if they lack access.

options.text: They receive a push notification
options.explanation: Access restrictions prevent notifications.

options.text: They are not notified
options.explanation: Notifications are only sent to users who have access to the page.

questions.question: You want to customize how inline comments appear (Default vs Minimal). Where do you go?

options.text: Customize page > Inline comments
options.explanation: This is the correct path to switch between Default and Minimal comment display.

options.text: Page settings > Comments
options.explanation: There is no specific 'Page settings > Comments' menu.

options.text: View options > Comment layout
options.explanation: Comment layout is a page customization setting.

options.text: Edit page > Comment settings
options.explanation: Settings are accessed via the ••• menu in the page header.

questions.question: What happens if you rename a page that has been @-mentioned in other pages?

options.text: The mention is removed
options.explanation: The mention text updates automatically to reflect the new title.

options.text: The mention turns into a link
options.explanation: It remains a mention, but with the updated text.

options.text: The mention becomes unlinked
options.explanation: The link remains intact and updates automatically.

options.text: The mention text updates automatically
options.explanation: The new title will automatically reflect in wherever the page is mentioned.

questions.question: How do you view where a specific comment was made on a page from the comments pane?

options.text: The pane automatically highlights the text
options.explanation: The pane does not automatically highlight; you must click to jump to the location.

options.text: Click on the comment to see its location
options.explanation: Clicking a comment in the pane navigates you to the specific line or block.

options.text: You have to re-open the page from the sidebar
options.explanation: You can stay in the same page and just jump to the comment.

options.text: The pane only shows the comment text
options.explanation: The pane provides context and navigation to the comment.

questions.question: In a timeline database view, how can you leave a comment on the database page?

options.text: Right-click the page and select Comment
options.explanation: Right-click is not available in timeline view.

options.text: Select >> at the left of the timeline to show the table, then Comment
options.explanation: Timeline view hides the table by default; you must expand it to see the options.

options.text: Hover over the timeline bar and select Comment
options.explanation: You cannot comment directly on the timeline bar.

options.text: Click the comment icon in the header
options.explanation: The header options are different in timeline view.
